data.title: Enforce measurable interaction timing and design quality signals in design review
data.description:
Incorporate concrete, measurable UI/UX constraints into DSO's design review process as hard enforcement — not soft suggestions.

## Reference: ui-ux-pro-max-skill

### Interaction timing as enforced constraints (not guidelines)
ui-ux-pro-max-skill treats these as hard constraints checkable in code review:
- Animation durations: 150-300ms with platform-native easing
- Tap/click feedback latency: 80-150ms (ripple/opacity/elevation)
- Touch targets: 44×44pt minimum (Apple HIG) / 48×48dp minimum (Material Design)
- Micro-interactions: 150-300ms
- prefers-reduced-motion: mandatory, not optional

### No emoji as icons — enforced professional quality signal
The skill explicitly bans emoji as UI icons and requires professional SVG icon libraries (Heroicons, Lucide, Phosphor, USWDS icons). This is treated as a concrete, automatable code quality signal — not a style preference.

## What to Add to DSO Design Review

1. Add measurable interaction timing constraints to the design review checklist — reviewer should flag animations outside 150-300ms, missing tap feedback, and touch targets below platform minimums
2. Add an automated check for emoji used as icons in UI code (detectable via regex/AST)
3. Add prefers-reduced-motion support as a required review dimension
4. Make these constraints configurable per project via dso-config.conf (e.g., design.animation_max_ms=300, design.touch_target_min=44)
5. Integrate into the completion verifier for UI stories — verify timing and icon constraints are met before story closure
